dialogueChunks.intro: I can't believe it worked. {player_name}, they're leaving peacefully.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} packed up and headed north. No raid, no blood. {target_npc} watched from the watchtower and said one of them turned back and raised a hand: not a threat, just a farewell. {quest_focus} turned out to be true, and the settlement still doesn't know the full story. But nobody died.
dialogueChunks.outro: You walked into a situation that could have ended in massacre and found a way through {quest_threat}. I won't forget that.

playerResponses.text: A raised hand is a start. Maybe someday it'll be an open one.
It's done. Let's hope both sides remember this.
Just make sure the guards don't chase after them.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... the {enemy_type} elder sent something for you.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It's a carved token. {target_npc} says it means 'remembered friend' in their culture. The settlement is confused, some are angry, but most are relieved the fighting stopped. Some are even asking questions about why the {enemy_type} were here. {quest_focus} is changing how people think.
dialogueChunks.outro: You didn't just end a conflict. You planted a seed of understanding where {quest_threat} once ruled. Take this token and what little we can offer.

playerResponses.text: I'll carry this token with pride. Thank you.
Understanding takes time. But today was a good step.

dialogueChunks.intro: It's quiet now. A different kind of quiet than before: not the silence of fear, but the stillness after something heavy finally lifts.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} moderates made it back and convinced the war party to stand down. {target_npc} told me a small delegation came to the edge of {location} and left a bundle of {quest_item} at the border marker. A gift, not a theft. First time that's ever happened. {quest_focus} has become something new.
dialogueChunks.outro: I spent my whole life hating them. Now I don't know what I feel. But it's something better than {quest_threat}. Take this: you've earned it.

playerResponses.text: Something better than hate is a fine place to start.
Change is uncomfortable. But it beats the alternative.
